title: The End

jokes.text: Larry buying a car for his nanny from Lewis while he's still in the hospital bed
jokes.setup: Man in hospital bed conducting car sales
jokes.punchline: The casual business transaction in this inappropriate setting
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - turning a hospital visit into a business opportunity with characteristic awkwardness

jokes.text: Lewis refusing to give his kidney to his cousin: 'What, are you crazy? I'm not giving anybody my kidney. I just woke up. I'm gonna give things out now?'
jokes.setup: Someone asking about helping family member who needs kidney
jokes.punchline: Lewis's selfish logic presented as if it's perfectly reasonable
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - the selfishness is so blatant and the logic so twisted it becomes hilarious

jokes.text: Lewis comparing his coma to bear hibernation: 'You know those bears, they had it right. They go into hibernation, they come out like new bears.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why he feels so refreshed after his coma
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to hibernating bears with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd analogy delivered with complete conviction

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate DVD case system explanation
jokes.setup: Missing Sopranos DVD case
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly complex 'foolproof' system that clearly doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry neurosis - creating elaborate systems for simple tasks that inevitably fail

jokes.text: Larry dismissing 44-year friendship: 'I know him 44 years, but we're not really close friends. He's more of an acquaintance. Who gives a kidney to an acquaintance?'
jokes.setup: Discussing whether to give Lewis a kidney
jokes.punchline: Redefining a 44-year relationship as just an 'acquaintance'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - the casual dismissal of a decades-long friendship to avoid obligation is perfectly in character and absurd

jokes.text: Larry's airplane emergency exit panic: 'I cannot be of any help whatsoever in any kind of non-traditional landing or any traditional landing.'
jokes.setup: Flight attendant asking if he can help in emergency
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete honesty about his uselessness in any situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neurosis - his self-awareness about his limitations becomes the joke

jokes.text: Larry's casual racism with the flight attendant: 'I'm sorry, are you Chinese? You look a little Asian... You look maybe Thai?'
jokes.setup: Larry using expression 'it's Chinese to me'
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempts to clarify that escalate the offense
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry cringe - making a bad situation worse by trying to fix it

jokes.text: Larry's basketball choking analogy: 'I couldn't even hit the rim! 'Cause I was choking.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't handle pressure
jokes.punchline: The vivid image of completely missing the basketball rim
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's self-awareness about his limitations

jokes.text: Larry correctly guessing the flight attendant is Thai: 'You're not Chinese. You don't look Chinese. What are you, Thai? I think you're Thai. You're Thai? I knew it.'
jokes.setup: Larry's earlier offensive questioning about her ethnicity
jokes.punchline: His smug satisfaction at guessing correctly despite the inappropriate situation
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete obliviousness to social inappropriateness while feeling vindicated

jokes.text: The guy was a little nervous and jumpy. He was all over the place. Jumpy, fidgety. Fidgety guy? Kind of talked like that a little bit?
jokes.setup: Biological father describing Larry's adoptive father
jokes.punchline: Describing Larry's mannerisms exactly, which Larry recognizes
jokes.explanation: Nice irony - biological father unknowingly describing Larry's inherited traits in his adoptive father.

jokes.text: Larry's realization: 'Oh my god. I'm gentile.'
jokes.setup: Learning his biological parents are from England and Scotland
jokes.punchline: The dawning realization of his non-Jewish heritage
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - the simplicity of the line delivers maximum impact for Larry's identity crisis

jokes.text: Oh my god. I'm gentile.
jokes.setup: Learning his biological parents are from England/Scotland with name 'Cone'
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked realization about his true heritage
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff - Larry's Jewish identity being central to his character makes this revelation perfect. Great economy.

jokes.text: Ouch.
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning his friend told him blue book was $5000 less
jokes.punchline: Simple 'ouch' reaction revealing Larry was ripped off
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word reveals the entire situation. Good deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Mother's Christian advice: 'Give him the benefit of the doubt. That's what Jesus would do.'
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about being cheated
jokes.punchline: The gentle Christian response to Larry's Jewish neurosis
jokes.explanation: Nice culture clash - Christian forgiveness versus Larry's natural vindictiveness

jokes.text: Larry's confused attempt at sign of the cross: 'nose, toes... and watch, wallet'
jokes.setup: Trying to learn Christian customs
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding the gesture and making up his own version
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - trying to fit in but creating something completely wrong and absurd

jokes.text: Larry walking out during the sermon about generosity
jokes.setup: Sermon hitting too close to home about helping friends
jokes.punchline: Larry literally walking away from moral instruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy - Larry's physical rejection of moral guidance

jokes.text: Larry's transformation on the plane: 'You can depend on me, ma'am. I won't let you down. I got it covered.'
jokes.setup: Same flight attendant offering to move Larry from emergency exit
jokes.punchline: Complete personality reversal - now confident and helpful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character arc - Larry's Christian conversion creating complete behavioral opposite

jokes.text: Larry's dramatic kidney offer: 'You can have my kidney.'
jokes.setup: Lewis desperately needing a kidney
jokes.punchline: Larry's sudden heroic gesture after his Christian awakening
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete personality transformation paying off in dramatic gesture

jokes.text: What are you wearing? Is this a sweater vest?
jokes.setup: Larry returns home in Christian attire
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's confused reaction to his outfit change
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - Larry's sudden transformation into Christian dad look.

jokes.text: Larry's sudden desire for children: 'I want to have children. Lots of them.'
jokes.setup: Larry never wanted children before
jokes.punchline: Sudden enthusiasm for large family due to Christian influence
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete personality reversal continues with family planning

jokes.text: Hospital attendant's dark joke: 'He didn't watch over the last guy.'
jokes.setup: Larry saying God will watch over him during surgery
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to previous patient dying
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark hospital humor delivered with casual brutality

jokes.text: Attendant's response to putter story: 'You're not attached to your kidney?'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining Lewis won't loan his putter
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurd contrast with giving away an organ
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that perfectly highlights the absurd priorities - concise and devastating

jokes.text: That's fucked up, man.
jokes.setup: The kidney vs putter absurdity
jokes.punchline: Doctor's unprofessional but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Good punctuation line - doctor's professional mask slipping to express what audience thinks.

jokes.text: Larry telling Lewis about the putter conversation: 'Anything except loan him your putter.'
jokes.setup: Lewis promising to do anything for Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately exposing Lewis's hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry move - immediately creating awkwardness in a touching moment

jokes.text: Omar's revelation: 'The Cones, they're not your parents. You're not adopted.'
jokes.setup: Larry about to undergo surgery based on his Christian conversion
jokes.punchline: The complete reversal of his identity crisis
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection - perfect timing to create maximum chaos and character reversal

jokes.text: Larry's panicked escape from surgery: 'Get me the fuck out of here!'
jokes.setup: Realizing he's not adopted and doesn't need to be Christian
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversion to his true selfish nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry instantly reverting to type when his motivation disappears

jokes.text: You paid me $5,000 less than the blue book.
jokes.setup: Friend confronting Larry about the car sale
jokes.punchline: Confirming Larry was indeed ripped off
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution - confirms the car sale deception setup earlier.

jokes.text: Larry's philosophy: 'See? This is what you get from doing good deeds. I avoided good deeds my whole life. Turns out I was right.'
jokes.setup: Larry's near-death experience from trying to help
jokes.punchline: Concluding that his selfishness was justified all along
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry wisdom - turning a near-death experience into validation of his worst instincts

jokes.text: Rabbi's brutal honesty: 'Well god is apparently making an exception in your case.'
jokes.setup: Larry saying good people usually die young
jokes.punchline: Rabbi confirming Larry isn't good
jokes.explanation: Devastating deadpan delivery - the rabbi's brutal honesty delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: Larry's travel philosophy: 'The truth is, Rabbi, I can't stand packing.'
jokes.setup: Rabbi asking about travel regrets before death
jokes.punchline: Reducing profound regret to mundane inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry reduction of life's big questions to petty annoyances

jokes.text: Larry's final confession to Jeff: 'You use way too much mayo.'
jokes.setup: Death bed confession moment
jokes.punchline: Focusing on Jeff's sandwich-making technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-climax - profound moment reduced to sandwich criticism

jokes.text: Also... you really never should have let me do that Seinfeld 'tv guide' cover. I really looked like an asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing his deathbed confessions
jokes.punchline: Meta reference to Larry David's real TV Guide cover
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - referencing real Larry David publicity photo while maintaining character.

jokes.text: Larry's deathbed request to fool around: 'would it be okay if I fooled around a little... until you got there?'
jokes.setup: Tender goodbye moment with wife
jokes.punchline: Asking permission to cheat while she mourns
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry selfishness - turning his deathbed into an opportunity for infidelity

jokes.text: I fooled around a little... until you got there? / Sure.
jokes.setup: Larry on deathbed asking permission to fool around in afterlife
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's casual agreement to adultery after death
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny deathbed negotiation. Cheryl's casual agreement makes it even more absurd.

jokes.text: Larry's final words about the DVD system: 'I have a system.'
jokes.setup: Larry dying while defending his organizational method
jokes.punchline: His last words being about his flawed DVD system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character consistency - Larry's dying words being about his petty organizational obsessions

jokes.text: Marilyn Monroe being a Seinfeld fan in heaven
jokes.setup: Larry meeting his afterlife guides
jokes.punchline: Marilyn Monroe loving his show despite being dead before it aired
jokes.explanation: Fun meta joke about the show's cultural impact extending beyond death

jokes.text: Larry's mother appearing: 'What kind of schmuck are you?! Who goes around giving their kidney to people? Idiot!'
jokes.setup: Emotional reunion with deceased mother
jokes.punchline: She immediately criticizes his life choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect mother-son dynamic - even in death she's still disapproving and controlling

jokes.text: Ben Hogan's golf advice: 'You're moving your feet. You know that sex offender had a good tip for you.'
jokes.setup: Golf legend giving Larry advice
jokes.punchline: Referencing advice from a sex offender as valid golf instruction
jokes.explanation: Great callback that creates absurd juxtaposition between golf legend and criminal

jokes.text: So, if you're my guides, then how come you let me eat those seven pieces of pizza at camp? Remember, I got sick and had to go home?
jokes.setup: Larry questioning his spirit guides
jokes.punchline: Blaming them for childhood pizza overeating incident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - blaming cosmic forces for his own poor childhood decisions.

jokes.text: Fuck you. Fuck you. Fuck you! Fuck you. Fuck you.
jokes.setup: Argument about DVD system in heaven
jokes.punchline: Larry having a profanity-filled argument in the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Larry being argumentative even with celestial beings.

jokes.text: Larry getting kicked out of heaven for arguing: 'we've decided that you're not really ready'
jokes.setup: Larry arguing with his spiritual guides
jokes.punchline: Being literally too difficult for heaven to handle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept - Larry being so argumentative he gets rejected from the afterlife itself

jokes.text: Marilyn Monroe's goodbye: 'I was so looking forward to meeting you. I really love your sense of humor.'
jokes.setup: Larry being sent back from heaven
jokes.punchline: Even Marilyn Monroe appreciating Larry's comedy as he's rejected
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that validates Larry's comedy even as he's being rejected

jokes.text: Larry using the regular bathroom when wheelchair accessible is occupied: 'I had to duck in here for a second. It was a bit of an emergency.'
jokes.setup: Larry caught walking when he should be in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Immediately lying about his emergency to justify the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - immediately getting caught in a compromising situation and making it worse with lies

tmdb_overview: Larry finally learns the truth regarding his possible adoption, and makes his final decision about a kidney donation for Richard Lewis.
